content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 3 correctness gaps in the new prompt-contract parser/renderer: raw canonical headings inside must-do scope self-invalidates prompt creation, numbered lists inside extracted sections collapse into one item, and Markdown headings with trailing colons are not recognized as non-goal/acceptance markers.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:772-776` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:824-827` emit `Must-Do Scope` verbatim, but `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:158-188` treats any unindented canonical heading as a real contract section. A bead description that includes an example heading like `## Review Policy` or `## Milestone Summary` now makes the generated prompt fail its own validation path in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:385-394`, so `project create-from-bead` rejects otherwise valid bead text.
   Rationale: This is an outright regression: prompt generation can fail for descriptions that mention the contract headings in prose.
2. `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:550-569` only splits extracted section items on `- ` / `* ` bullets. If embedded `Non-goals` or `Acceptance Criteria` use a numbered Markdown list (`1. ...`, `2. ...`), `collect_section_items` merges the whole block into one bullet when `render_bead_task_prompt` re-emits it via `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:644-645` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:780-787`. That silently changes multiple criteria/non-goals into a single item.
   Rationale: Numbered lists are common Markdown; collapsing them loses scope boundaries and weakens the prompt contract.
3. `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:488-513` does not normalize a trailing `:` after Markdown headings. As a result, common forms like `## Non-goals:` or `## Acceptance Criteria:` are not recognized as section markers, so their contents stay in `Must-Do Scope` instead of populating the canonical sections. With an empty structured acceptance-criteria field, the prompt can even fall through to the `No explicit acceptance criteria were supplied.` fallback at `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:784-787` even though the description provided them.
   Rationale: This is a silent misparse of ordinary Markdown syntax that changes in-scope/out-of-scope boundaries.
